meta.purpose: Structured database of ALL UMA/Polymarket investigation findings
meta.methodology: Every claim tagged with confidence level and verification source

confidence_levels.PROVEN: On-chain tx hash verified via Etherscan API
confidence_levels.VERIFIED: Confirmed via subgraph query or multiple API calls
confidence_levels.CIRCUMSTANTIAL: Strong inference from multiple data points
confidence_levels.RETRACTED: Was claimed but proven wrong or misleading

HEADLINE_FINDING.summary: 86 of 89 DesignatedVotingV2 contracts from the factory are owned by a single Safe multisig (holdsRole exhaustively verified on ALL 89). These hold 6.76M UMA (38.3% of total 17.66M staked). Combined with Kevin Chan (1.52M) and Safe-funded wallets, estimated total bloc is ~50-62%. No one has ever published this.
HEADLINE_FINDING.novelty: This is the FIRST wallet-level forensic analysis of UMA DVM voting. Ogle's investigation (June 2025) covered ACX governance only — NOT UMA oracle voting.
HEADLINE_FINDING.estimated_total_bloc_percentage: 64.4% without BornTooLate, 70.9% with (live subgraph data April 21, 2026)
HEADLINE_FINDING.methodology_note: Factory is permissionless (36 unique callers). holdsRole(0, Safe) called on ALL 89 contracts. Result: 86 TRUE (Safe-owned), 3 FALSE (1 owned by Risk Labs: Deployer directly, 2 by independent users). Function selector: 0x7cdc1cb9 (keccak256 of 'holdsRole(uint256,address)').

deployment_timeline.2023: 51 contracts (34 in Feb alone, rest through November)

top_15_contracts_by_stake.note: Largest initial allocation from Mar 2 tx

ownership_verification.method: holdsRole(0, Safe) via eth_call on ALL 89 factory contracts. Selector: 0x7cdc1cb9.
ownership_verification.result: 86 TRUE (Safe-owned), 3 FALSE. FALSE contracts: #1 owned by Risk Labs: Deployer (0x9a8f92a8), #56 owned by 0xb51d0852 (independent), #78 owned by 0x1076188b (independent).

designated_voting_v2_system.implication: 86 of 89 factory contracts are owned by 1 Safe multisig signed by Hart Lambur (holdsRole exhaustively verified on ALL 89). Factory is permissionless (36 unique callers) but 96.6% of output is Safe-controlled.
designated_voting_v2_system.confidence: PROVEN (holdsRole verified on all 89 contracts — 86 TRUE, 3 FALSE)

acx_controversy.summary: Same wallet (maxodds.eth) cast 44% of YES vote weight on Across Protocol proposals moving $23M from DAO treasury to Risk Labs. Without insider bloc, proposal would have failed quorum. Exposed by Ogle June 2025. Hart Lambur confirmed wallet belongs to Kevin Chan but argued grants were legitimate.
acx_controversy.connection: maxodds.eth IS 0xd2a78bb8 — the SAME address that is #1 UMA DVM voter with 1.52M staked. Same conflict-of-interest pattern, different protocol.
acx_controversy.legal_status: Allegations of insider governance capture / self-dealing. NOT a criminal conviction. Undisputed facts: the wallet, the vote weight, and the team-to-team fund transfer.

funding_sources.note: Kraken 142 is a Kraken exchange hot wallet - someone withdrew from Kraken

reward_destination.pattern: 100% forwarded within minutes, gas funded by Coinbase 10 internal tx

borntoolate.role: #2 UMA voter, called 'unprecedented attacker' by Polymarket March 2025

delegate_bot_af4c.key_insight: Capital migrated from 0xbd9e to 0xeaf0 in Mar 2026, keeping same delegate bot. Same money, new wrapper.

delegate_bot_safe_itself.type: Gnosis Safe acting AS delegate for 34 wallets
delegate_bot_safe_itself.note: The Safe itself receives delegation from 34 wallets. This means the Safe BOTH owns 89 DV2 contracts AND receives delegation from 34 additional wallets.

kevin_chan_treasurer.note: Company Treasurer voting on company's own oracle outcomes

GRAND_TOTAL.note: Risk Labs + Treasurer hold 64.4% of ALL staked UMA. SPAT threshold is 65% (raised from 50% in May 2025). Bloc alone is just under SPAT. With BornTooLate (1.15M), reaches 70.9% — well above threshold. Before May 2025, exceeded the then-50% threshold for years.
GRAND_TOTAL.data_source: Live UMA V2 Subgraph query, April 21, 2026

what_is_public_knowledge: UMA voting concentrated in few wallets (surface-level media reports)
BornTooLate.eth attack on Ukraine market (extensively covered)
Kevin Chan = Treasurer of Risk Labs (public LinkedIn/YouTube)
UMA's slashing mechanism punishes dissent (UMA's own docs)
Polymarket bypasses UMA for US users (CFTC filings)
UMIP-189 whitelist restricted proposers (The Block, Aug 2025)
what_is_NOVEL_in_our_research: 89 DesignatedVotingV2 contracts ALL owned by same Safe = 6.76M UMA (38% of stake)
Kevin Chan identified as #1 UMA DVM voter (only known for ACX governance before)
Risk Labs Safe -> voting wallet funding chain for UMA DVM (only traced for ACX before)
Delegate bot 0xaf4c pattern connecting Safe-funded wallets
Total Risk Labs voting control: 64.4% of all staked UMA
BornTooLate.eth funding trace (Huobi -> single-use intermediary -> BTL)
99.35% vote agreement between Kevin Chan and Wallet #3 across 2,614 votes
Systematic voter concentration analysis across ALL dispute rounds
The DVM security model (CoC > PfC) is violated: insiders already above corruption threshold

retracted_claims.claim: Shared aggregator 0xa9d1e08c proves BornTooLate connected to Risk Labs
retracted_claims.reason: 0xa9d1e08c is Coinbase 10 - a massive exchange hot wallet used by millions. Multiple parties depositing to Coinbase does NOT prove connection.
retracted_claims.what_IS_true: BornTooLate, Wallet #3, and Wallet #5 all cash out through Coinbase. This shows same operational pattern but NOT necessarily same entity.

retracted_claims.claim: Safe is labeled 'Risk Labs Treasury' on Etherscan
retracted_claims.reason: Etherscan labels it only as 'Smart Account by Safe'. We prove the Risk Labs connection through getOwners() + hal2001.eth funding chain.

retracted_claims.claim: 10 DesignatedVotingV2 contracts all voting from one Safe
retracted_claims.reason: CORRECTED: It's actually 89 contracts, 68 with active stake, 55 actively voting. The original '10' was a dramatic undercount. The real number is far worse.
retracted_claims.correction: Updated from '10 contracts' to '89 contracts' based on factory contract analysis

gas_rebate_to_borntoolate.summary: Risk Labs Safe funds BornTooLate's voting gas through intermediary 0x774bFF0C via Disperse.app
gas_rebate_to_borntoolate.chain: Risk Labs Safe (0x8180d59b) → Gas Distributor (0x774bFF0CCD2f7546B67D4071DEF9Ba154bA549B1) → Disperse.app (0xD152f549) → BornTooLate (0xfc204fc)
gas_rebate_to_borntoolate.implication: Risk Labs pays BornTooLate's gas fees for voting. This does NOT prove BornTooLate is an insider, but it proves Risk Labs directly subsidizes this wallet's participation in the oracle.

round_10280_ceasefire_vote.hart_lambur_votingv2: ZERO activity — no commits, no reveals
round_10280_ceasefire_vote.technical_note: Subgraph RevealedVote.time stores REQUEST timestamp, not reveal TX time. Etherscan event logs used for actual reveal times.

open_questions: Who is BornTooLate? (Funding chain goes cold at Huobi exchange)
Are the 34 wallets delegating to the Safe ALSO Safe-funded? (Would increase total control)
What is the third delegate bot 0xe36b (301K UMA, 5 delegators) - who runs it?
Does Hart Lambur's Polygon activity (5 proposePrice calls) correlate with specific disputed markets?
Can we identify specific Polymarket whale wallets that held NO on ceasefire market?
What's the overlap between wallets voting in ALL controversial disputes vs routine ones?

security_model_breakdown.uma_claim: Cost of Corruption (CoC) must exceed Profit from Corruption (PfC)
security_model_breakdown.uma_assumption: No single entity controls enough UMA to manipulate votes

reality.pre_may_2025: Bloc exceeded the then-50% threshold for over two years
reality.profit_from_corruption: Unlimited (they decide outcomes of markets worth $99.5M-$280M each)
reality.conclusion: The security model's assumption of distributed ownership is challenged. The bloc is just below SPAT alone but above it with aligned voters. CoC = $0 because they never need to BUY tokens to influence.

slashing_creates_herding.mechanism: Vote with minority = lose 0.1% of stake per round
slashing_creates_herding.effect: Rational voters MUST vote with the majority (Risk Labs bloc) or lose money
slashing_creates_herding.result: Dissent is economically punished, conformity is rewarded
slashing_creates_herding.analogy: Like a jury system where disagreeing with the judge costs you money

security_model_breakdown.confidence: VERIFIED (from UMA whitepaper + on-chain staking data)
